message:
feat(core,func): thread CollationRegistry through ORDER BY and fix JOIN WHERE subquery evaluation

Thread the CollationRegistry through sort_rows_by_order_terms so that
ORDER BY ... COLLATE <name> and column-level collation declarations are
honored during result sorting. Previously, sort_rows_by_order_terms had
no access to the registry and silently fell back to BINARY comparison
for all collated columns.

Also fix JOIN materialization to use the connection-level subquery
evaluator (eval_expr_with_subqueries) instead of eval_join_predicate
for the final WHERE pass. This allows correlated EXISTS/NOT EXISTS
subqueries in JOIN WHERE clauses to work correctly.

Key changes:
- connection.rs: sort_rows_by_order_terms takes &Mutex<CollationRegistry>,
  resolves per-term collation from Expr::Collate or column metadata,
  uses cmp_sqlite_values_collated; all 7 call sites updated; JOIN WHERE
  uses eval_expr_with_subqueries + is_sqlite_truthy; test fixes for
  bd-rjc pager view and collation join query; new test for JOIN WHERE
  NOT EXISTS
- collation.rs: Debug impl for CollationRegistry; formatting cleanup
  in names() method
- engine.rs: mark unused SorterCursor::new with #[cfg(test)]

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
